title: Interfaces, Dynamic Types, Errors, and API Boundaries · Program Defense
summary: A notification service must accept narrow capabilities, preserve error causes, and distinguish nil interfaces from typed nil values. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a capability-oriented notification boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.
Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.
Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Api Capability Boundaries
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Notifier case 5 receives a typed-nil implementation, wraps a sentinel timeout, joins cleanup failure, and panics in one isolated plugin. Preserve dynamic-type evidence, error causes, resource ownership, and the narrow recover boundary. Competency probe: Place interfaces at consumer boundaries, return concrete useful types, and evaluate mocks, adapters, and evolution cost.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Api Capability Boundaries.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for place interfaces at consumer boundaries, return concrete useful types, and evaluate mocks, adapters, and evolution cost.
